summary: Joel Spolsky briefly announces two new FogBugz 4.0 features — Bayesian spam filtering and a 'snippets' feature for canned responses — that emerged from Fog Creek's internal use of their own product for handling company email. The post serves as a teaser directing readers to a longer article about the FogBugz 4.0 development process.
key_points: Fog Creek uses FogBugz internally to handle company email, practicing 'dogfooding'
Internal usage directly motivated two new features: Bayesian spam filtering and message snippets
Dogfooding is presented as a reliable product development methodology for discovering needed features
money_quotes: the process of using FogBugz ourselves ("eating our own dogfood") motivated us to add Bayesian spam filtering, and a "snippets" feature to make it easy to enter common phrases and even entire messages in replies to frequently-asked questions
key_insight: Using your own product internally (dogfooding) is an effective way to discover and prioritize features that real users need.
